mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in U.S. industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, signaling robust economic activity that drives corporate earnings expectations higher and pushes S&P 500 forward returns positive over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO's 12-month growth rate loses its positive correlation with SPX 60-day forward returns (rolling correlation < 0.1 for two consecutive years) or the spread between average returns in high-growth vs. low-growth periods fails to exceed 1% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate falling below 4.0% signals a critically tight labor market where wage pressures and margin compression begin to erode corporate profitability, leading the S&P 500 to underperform over the next month as investors price in rising recession risk.
failure_condition: If UNRATE < 4.0% and SPX 20-day forward return is positive more than 60% of the time (substantially above baseline), or if any prolonged period (12+ months) below 4% fails to produce a net negative cumulative return.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts and retail sales signal a synchronized contraction in consumer and investment demand that foreshadows an economic slowdown, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over a quarterly horizon as earnings revisions decline.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM decline > 2% AND RSAFS MoM decline > 1%
failure_condition: When both proxies decline simultaneously, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the joint signal's Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over any three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market regime (unemployment below 4.0%), any acceleration in headline consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise interest rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly increases above 0.2% produce positive SPX returns in more than 60% of occurrences during tight labor markets, or the regime-dependent signal decouples (Rank IC < 0.02) over two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent strength in retail and food services sales indicates robust consumer spending, which drives revenue growth for S&P 500 companies and supports positive equity returns over a monthly horizon as earnings momentum builds.
failure_condition: RSAFS year-over-year growth rate has a non-positive correlation with SPX 20-day forward returns, or the top quintile of RSAFS growth fails to outperform the bottom quintile by at least 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden upward shift in the federal funds rate, reflecting a hawkish policy surprise or accelerating tightening, triggers an immediate negative repricing of equities as borrowing costs rise and growth expectations are revised lower, leading to S&P 500 declines over a short one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Following FOMC rate hikes (FEDFUNDS increasing), 5-day SPX returns are positive in 70% or more of occurrences, or the signal's win rate does not exceed 55% over any three-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with rising consumer prices creates stagflationary pressure: tight labor markets push up wages and inflation forces the Fed to tighten, squeezing corporate margins and lowering equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE MoM decline > 0.1pp AND CPIAUCSL MoM increase > 0.2%
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative less than 55% of the time, or the joint signal's cumulative return is positive over any five-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment (above 6.0%), an uptick in industrial production signals the onset of a recovery as factories ramp up output and inventory restocking begins, driving corporate earnings improvement and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises >0.5% MoM while UNRATE >6.0% and SPX 60-day forward returns are negative more than half the time, or if the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.3 over any 10-year period.
